enforced.mr_name: RNEXT '=' alias roundtrip preserves alignment semantics
enforced.oracle: Toggling RNEXT between the explicit RNAME and the '=' alias (when RNEXT==RNAME) should produce semantically identical records. The canonical normalizer canonicalizes '=' to explicit RNAME, so normalized output of x and T(x) should match.

evidence.quote: This field is set as '*' when the information is unavailable, and set as '=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — uppercasing SEQ is semantics-preserving because the SAM spec states no assumptions can be made on letter cases.

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when the sum of query-consuming CIGAR operators (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ); silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: SEQ case normalization preserves sequence query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T uppercases SEQ field. SAMv1 §1.4.10 states 'No assumptions can be made on the letter cases' for SEQ, so case changes are semantics-preserving and query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR. An `=' denotes the base is identical to the reference base. No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.mr_name: Header record reordering preserves alignment query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T reorders @SQ header lines. The alignment records' mandatory fields (RNAME, POS, CIGAR, etc.) are unchanged, so all query methods on parsed alignment records must return identical values.

evidence.quote: SAM stands for Sequence Alignment/Map format. It is a TAB-delimited text format consisting of a header section, which is optional, and an alignment section. If present, the header must be prior to the alignments. Header lines start with ` @', while alignment lines do not. Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias canonicalization preserves query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T toggles RNEXT between explicit RNAME and '=' alias. SAMv1 §1.4.7 explicitly allows '=' as a synonym for 'same as RNAME', so this is semantics-preserving and query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. ... This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation preserves core query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T shuffles optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields (columns 12+) while keeping the 11 mandatory fields fixed. Core alignment data is unchanged, so query methods on parsed records must return identical values.

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @HD lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled SAM produces identical data structures; the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within the @HD line does not affect the parsed result.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @SQ lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled SAM produces identical data structures; the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ line does not affect the parsed result.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @RG lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled SAM produces identical data structures; the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within each @RG line does not affect the parsed result.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @PG lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled SAM produces identical data structures; the order of TAG:VALUE pairs within each @PG line does not affect the parsed result.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order invariance on alignment records
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the permuted SAM produces identical data structures; the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment line does not affect the parsed result.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the shuffled SAM produces identical data structures; the order of @CO free-text comment lines in the header does not affect the parsed result.

evidence.quote: Each header line begins with the character `@' followed by one of the two-letter header record type codes defined in this section. In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE'... Thus header lines match /94@(HD|SQ|RG|PG)(92t[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]:[ -126]+)+$/ or /94@CO92t.*/.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format and match a different regex pattern, confirming they are free-text comments with no ordering semantics imposed by the spec.

quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original SAM and the reordered SAM produces identical data structures; the order of @SQ lines in the header does not affect the parsed result (except for coordinate-sorted files where @SQ order defines sort key ordering, but the parsed data content is identical).

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The @SQ line order defines the sort key ordering for coordinate-sorted files (@HD SO:coordinate), so while the parsed data content is invariant, the semantic interpretation of sort order may change. This MR tests that parsers correctly load all @SQ data regardless of declaration order.

quarantine.mr_name: Splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves alignment
quarantine.oracle: Splitting a CIGAR operation into two identical operations (10M -> 4M6M) or merging adjacent identical operations (4M6M -> 10M) preserves the total query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths. The canonical normalizer merges adjacent identical ops, so normalized output of x and T(x) should match.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (advisory), not 'must'. Some parsers may not normalize adjacent identical ops, but the total consumed lengths are preserved.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — zero-length CIGAR ops contribute nothing to alignment and removing them (merging adjacent same-type ops) yields a semantically equivalent CIGAR string.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The SAM spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops, but the Recommended Practice says adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.

quarantine.mr_name: Flag-bit exclusivity violation: unmapped flag set on mapped record
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on a record where FLAG 0x4 is set but RNAME != '*' and POS > 0; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' rather than a strict MUST NOT; the Recommended Practice uses 'should' (advisory) rather than 'must'. However, the combination of a mapped RNAME/POS with the unmapped flag creates an inherent contradiction that conformant parsers should reject.

quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when an optional tag's TYPE character is not one of the legal values (A, i, f, Z, H, B); silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: In an optional field, TYPE is a single case-sensitive letter which defines the format of VALUE:

A & [!-126] & Printable character \\ i & [-+]?[0-9]+ & Signed integer\\ f & [-+]?[0-9]*92.?[0-9]+([eE][-+]?[0-9]+)? & Single-precision floating number \\ Z & [\,\,\,!-126]* & Printable string, including space\\ H & ([0-9A-F][0-9A-F])* & Byte array in the Hex format\\ B & [cCsSiIf](,[-+]?[0-9]*92.?[0-9]+([eE][-+]?[0-9]+)?)* & Integer or numeric array\\

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines the valid TYPE letters (A, i, f, Z, H, B) but does not explicitly state that other letters MUST be rejected; however, the definitional table is exhaustive, implying any other character is invalid.

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when two reads of a paired template carry same-signed TLEN values; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: TLEN: observed Template LENgth. ... The absolute value of TLEN equals the number of bases from the leftmost mapped base to the rightmost mapped base, inclusive. For a paired-end read, the TLEN of the first read is positive, and the TLEN of the second read is negative.

ambiguity_flags: The TLEN sign convention is described in the spec but the exact wording about 'first read positive, second read negative' is in the spec's prose description. The spec also notes that TLEN definitions are implementation-defined for overlapping reads.

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with matching SQ LN preserves coordinate validity
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing @SQ LN by +shift, every record's POS + CIGAR_ref_consume ≤ new LN. The mapping coordinates remain valid relative to the reference sequence length.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: 4 &  POS & Int & $[0,\,2^31-1]$ & 1-based leftmost mapping POSition \\

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be unmapped' (advisory, not mandatory) when POS+CIGAR exceeds LN; the transform enforces the stricter invariant that POS+CIGAR never exceeds LN after shifting.

quarantine.oracle: After SAM→BAM→SAM round-trip, every alignment record's POS field (1-based leftmost mapping position) is identical to the original. The 1-based coordinate system is preserved through binary encoding/decoding.

evidence.quote: 4 &  POS & Int & $[0,\,2^31-1]$ & 1-based leftmost mapping POSition \\

1-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [3,7]. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

ambiguity_flags: BAM encoding may canonicalize certain edge cases (e.g., zero-length CIGAR ops) which could affect coordinate interpretation indirectly.

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record normalization preserves POS=0 sentinel
quarantine.oracle: After normalizing unmapped records (setting MAPQ=255), the POS field for records with FLAG 0x4 remains unchanged. For records where RNAME='*' and POS=0 (unplaced unmapped), these sentinel values are preserved. The 1-based coordinate system's sentinel value 0 for 'no coordinate' is invariant.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: In contrast, unplaced unmapped reads have~`*' and~0 for  RNAME and~ POS.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be set' (advisory) for unmapped POS=0; the transform only normalizes MAPQ, not POS, so POS sentinel values are preserved as-is.

quarantine.oracle: After SAM→CRAM→SAM round-trip, every alignment record's POS field (1-based leftmost mapping position) is identical to the original. The 1-based coordinate system is preserved through CRAM lossless encoding/decoding.

evidence.quote: 4 &  POS & Int & $[0,\,2^31-1]$ & 1-based leftmost mapping POSition \\

1-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [3,7]. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may canonicalize =/X to M in CIGAR, which could affect coordinate interpretation if the SUT treats =/X differently from M for coordinate span calculation.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal preserves sequence query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T strips zero-length CIGAR ops and merges adjacent same-type ops. SAMv1 §1.4.6 doesn't forbid 0-length ops but the SAM Recommended Practice (p134) says 'adjacent CIGAR operations should be different'. The total query-consuming length is preserved, so query methods must return identical values.

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

ambiguity_flags: SAM Recommended Practice says 'adjacent CIGAR operations should be different' — this is a SHOULD, not a MUST, so zero-length ops are technically valid but not recommended.
